29.reason: [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem states that terms with zero coefficient are omitted, not that input ends with exponent 0. This causes premature termination if a term has exponent 0 (constant term) that is non-zero.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem states that terms with zero coefficient are omitted, not that input ends with exponent 0. This causes premature termination if a term has exponent 0 (constant term) that is non-zero.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem states that terms with zero coefficient are omitted, not that input ends with exponent 0. This causes premature termination if a term has exponent 0 (constant term) that is not the last term or if the polynomial doesn't end with x^0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem states that terms with zero coefficient are omitted, not that exponent 0 marks end of input. This causes premature termination if constant term (exponent 0) appears before end of line.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem allows constant term (exponent 0) as valid input. This causes premature termination and misses the last term if it's a constant term.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop breaks when exponent is 0, but the problem states that terms with zero coefficient are omitted, not that exponent 0 marks end of input. This causes premature termination if constant term (exponent 0) appears before end of line.]

38.reason: [Same error as above: incorrectly assumes input ends when exponent is 0, which is not the case per problem description. Input ends with newline, not a zero exponent.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Same error as above: incorrectly assumes exponent 0 terminates input.]

47.reason: [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j, not <=, because i and j point to the first unused index after reading. This accesses uninitialized data including the term with exponent 0 that caused loop exit.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j, not <=, because i and j point to the first unused index after reading. This accesses uninitialized data including the term with exponent 0 that caused loop exit.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop bounds are incorrect: should be i1 < i and j1 < j, not <=, because i and j point to the first unused index after the last valid term. This accesses uninitialized data (including the term with exponent 0 that caused loop exit).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j because i and j are counts of terms read before encountering exponent 0, but the last valid index is i-1 and j-1. Also, since the input reading stops at exponent 0, the constant term (if present) is not included in dxs1/dxs2 arrays.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j because i and j point to the first unused index after reading, not inclusive. Also, since the previous loops may have skipped the exponent-0 term, the data itself is incomplete.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop bounds are off-by-one: should be i1 < i and j1 < j because i and j are counts of terms, not inclusive indices. This accesses uninitialized memory beyond valid terms.]

96.reason: [The merging logic is flawed: it only merges consecutive duplicates and stops at j < n-1, so the last element is never merged. Also, it doesn't handle cases where same exponents are not adjacent after sorting (though sort should make them adjacent, the loop condition j < n-1 skips the last possible merge).]

Also, it doesn't handle cases where same exponents are not adjacent after sorting (though sort should make them adjacent, the loop condition j < n-1 skips the last possible merge).]

Also, it doesn't handle non-consecutive duplicate exponents after sorting (though sort should make them consecutive, the loop condition j < n-1 skips the last possible merge).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The merging logic is flawed: it only merges consecutive duplicates and stops at j < n-1, so the last element is never merged. Also, it doesn't handle non-consecutive duplicate exponents after sorting correctly because the inner loop condition limits j to n-2.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [The merging logic is flawed: it only merges consecutive duplicates and stops at j < n-1, so the last element is never merged even if it has the same exponent as a[i]. Also, it doesn't handle non-consecutive duplicates because the array may not be fully sorted before merging (but sort() is called before hb(), so that part is okay). However, the condition j < n-1 prevents checking the last element.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The merging logic is flawed: it only merges consecutive duplicates and stops at j < n-1, so last element may not be merged; also doesn't handle non-consecutive duplicates after sorting correctly due to incomplete merging.]
